questions.title: Config location in YAML
questions.context: Current config has top-level sections: imap, server, rules, review. The review section nests moveTracking under it. Action folders are a new top-level feature — do they nest under an existing section or get their own?

options.label: Top-level `actionFolders` section
options.description: Parallel to imap/server/rules/review. Clean separation, easy to find. Research summary recommends this.

options.label: Nested under `review` section
options.description: Action folders are related to review workflow. Groups monitoring-related config together. But action folders aren’t really about Review.

questions.title: Default folder prefix
questions.context: The PRD specifies an `Actions/` prefix with 4 sub-folders (VIP Sender, Block Sender, Undo VIP, Unblock Sender). The prefix is configurable per CONF-01. What should the default prefix be?

options.label: `Actions`
options.description: Clean, generic, matches PRD examples. Creates Actions/VIP Sender, Actions/Block Sender, etc.

options.label: `Mail Manager`
options.description: Makes it clear these folders belong to this application. Creates Mail Manager/VIP Sender, etc.

options.label: `_Actions` (underscore prefix)
options.description: Sorts to top of folder list in most mail clients. Visually distinct from user folders.

questions.title: Default sub-folder names
questions.context: PRD uses: VIP Sender, Block Sender, Undo VIP, Unblock Sender. These are user-facing names visible in the mail client. Are these the right defaults or should they be different?

options.label: Keep PRD names as-is
options.description: VIP Sender, Block Sender, Undo VIP, Unblock Sender — action-oriented, clear what happens

options.label: Shorter names
options.description: VIP, Block, Undo VIP, Unblock — terser, less cluttered in sidebar

options.label: Emoji-prefixed for visibility
options.description: ⭐ VIP Sender, 🚫 Block Sender, etc. — stands out visually in mail client but may not render everywhere

questions.title: Individual folder name configurability
questions.context: CONF-01 says prefix AND individual folder names are configurable. Should users be able to rename individual action folders (e.g. change 'VIP Sender' to 'Priority'), or is prefix-only enough?

options.label: Both prefix and individual names configurable
options.description: Full flexibility. Schema has `prefix` + per-folder `name` overrides. More config surface but matches CONF-01 exactly.

options.label: Prefix only — sub-folder names are fixed
options.description: Simpler config. Users can still change the parent folder name. Sub-folder names are well-known constants.

questions.title: Enable/disable behavior
questions.context: CONF-02 requires action folders can be enabled/disabled. MoveTracker uses a simple `enabled: boolean` that skips `start()`. The question is what 'disabled' means for folders that already exist on the IMAP server.

options.label: Disabled = don’t create, don’t monitor, ignore existing
options.description: If folders already exist on server, they’re left alone but not watched. Simplest behavior. Re-enabling just starts monitoring.

options.label: Disabled = don’t create, don’t monitor, warn if messages found
options.description: Same as (a) but on startup, log a warning if disabled action folders contain messages. Prevents silent message loss.

questions.title: Default poll interval
questions.context: CONF-03 requires configurable poll interval. Research recommends 15 seconds. Existing intervals: IMAP poll=60s, MoveTracker=30s, Sweep=6h. Action folders should be responsive since the user just dragged a message.

options.label: 15 seconds (research recommendation)
options.description: Good balance — user waits ~7.5s average after drag. STATUS is lightweight (no mailbox switch). Same as MoveTracker order of magnitude.

options.label: 10 seconds
options.description: More responsive (~5s average wait). Still lightweight. More chatty on the IMAP connection.

options.label: 30 seconds (match MoveTracker)
options.description: Conservative. ~15s average wait. Less server load but user might wonder if it’s working.

questions.title: When to create folders
questions.context: FOLD-01 says create on startup. Current codebase creates folders lazily (on move failure, retry-once pattern in actions/index.ts). The question is whether action folders should be eagerly created at startup or lazily on first use.

options.label: Eager creation at startup
options.description: Folders exist immediately after first run. User sees them in mail client right away. Matches FOLD-01 exactly. Fails fast if IMAP permissions are wrong.

options.label: Lazy creation on first poll
options.description: Folders created when monitoring starts, not during startup. Slight delay but doesn’t block startup on folder creation errors.

questions.title: Handling already-exists on creation
questions.context: If folders already exist (e.g., second startup, or user pre-created them), `mailboxCreate` will throw. Need to handle gracefully. imapflow’s error for already-exists is a standard IMAP NO response.

options.label: Try create, catch already-exists, log debug, continue
options.description: Simple try/catch. Log at debug level since this is the normal case after first run. No overhead of checking first.

options.label: Check existence first via status(), only create if missing
options.description: Cleaner — no error thrown in normal case. But adds an extra IMAP call per folder on every startup.

questions.title: Folder creation failure handling
questions.context: If folder creation fails (permissions, server error), what should happen? Action folders can’t function without the IMAP folders existing.

options.label: Log error, disable action folder monitoring, continue startup
options.description: Graceful degradation. Rest of the app works fine. User sees error in logs. Can fix IMAP permissions and restart.

options.label: Log error, retry on next poll cycle
options.description: Self-healing — if it’s a transient IMAP error, the next poll will try again. But could spam logs with repeated failures.

options.label: Fatal error, abort startup
options.description: If action folders are enabled but can’t be created, it’s a config/permission problem that won’t fix itself. Forces the user to address it.

questions.title: Where in startup sequence
questions.context: Current startup: config → IMAP connect → envelope discovery → sweeper/batch start → MoveTracker start. Action folder creation and monitoring need to fit somewhere in this sequence.

options.label: After IMAP connect, before sweeper/MoveTracker
options.description: Create folders right after IMAP is up. Ensures folders exist before anything else runs. Natural position since it’s a one-time setup.

options.label: After all existing components start
options.description: Don’t change the existing startup order. Action folders are the last thing to initialize. Safest for existing behavior.

questions.title: Config hot-reload behavior
questions.context: ConfigRepository supports hot-reload via onChange callbacks (onRulesChange, onImapConfigChange, onReviewConfigChange). If the user changes action folder config, what should happen?

options.label: Add onActionFolderConfigChange callback
options.description: Follows established pattern. On change: stop polling, re-read config, create any new folders, restart polling. Full consistency.

options.label: Require restart for action folder config changes
options.description: Simpler. Action folder config is a startup concern. Changing folder names mid-run is weird anyway. Just document ‘restart required’.

questions.title: Scope of Phase 17 deliverable
questions.context: Phase 17 covers CONF-01, CONF-02, CONF-03, FOLD-01. The question is whether this phase just adds config + folder creation, or also sets up the polling timer skeleton (without actual processing, which is Phase 19).

options.label: Config + folder creation only
options.description: Strictly what the requirements say. Schema validation, defaults, IMAP folder creation on startup. No timer, no processor skeleton. Clean boundary.

options.label: Config + folder creation + empty processor skeleton
options.description: Add the ActionFolderProcessor class with poll timer that does nothing yet. Phase 18/19 fill in the behavior. Gives something to wire tests against.
